user_query:
You are a senior digital workplace consultant onboarding a Fortune-500 client. You have been given a company profile. Your job is to design and build a complete, populated intranet hub — not a skeleton. A real internal workspace has density: multiple articles per channel, FAQs where they make sense, highlights, policies, leadership updates, and hub-wide polish. You will use every relevant feature of the Diggspace platform. COMPANY PROFILE: --- COMPANY PROFILE — APPLE INC. Source: Public information compiled for workspace onboarding purposes COMPANY OVERVIEW Legal name: Apple Inc. Founded: April 1, 1976 Headquarters: One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014, USA CEO: Tim Cook (since 2011) Employees: ~160,000 worldwide (full-time) Revenue (FY2024): $391 billion USD Fiscal year end: Last Saturday of September Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories, and sells a variety of related services. It is one of the world's most valuable companies by market capitalisation and is widely regarded as one of the most influential technology companies in history. BRAND IDENTITY Apple's brand is defined by simplicity, premium quality, and human-centred design. The visual language is clean, minimal, and typographically precise. Primary brand colors: - Apple Blue: #0071E3 (primary — CTAs, interactive elements, headers) - Pure White: #FFFFFF (primary contrast — text on blue) - Space Black: #1D1D1F (secondary — depth, dark surfaces, typography) - Signal Red: #FF3B30 (warn — alerts, warnings, product accents) - Signal Red contrast: #FFFFFF Workspace color mapping (use these when building the hub): primary_color: #0071E3 primary_color_contrast: #FFFFFF secondary_color: #1D1D1F warn_color: #FF3B30 warn_color_contrast: #FFFFFF Typography: San Francisco (proprietary), Helvetica Neue as fallback. Design philosophy: "Design is not just what it looks like. Design is how it works." — Steve Jobs Website: https://www.apple.com Careers: https://www.apple.com/careers Newsroom: https://www.apple.com/newsroom Privacy: https://www.apple.com/privacy Investor Relations: https://investor.apple.com Accessibility: https://www.apple.com/accessibility Developer: https://developer.apple.com Support: https://support.apple.com Social media: Twitter/X: https://twitter.com/Apple YouTube: https://youtube.com/Apple LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/apple BUSINESS DIVISIONS & PRODUCTS Apple operates through five reportable segments: 1. iPhone — The world's leading smartphone. Introduced in 2007 by Steve Jobs. iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15, iPhone SE are current flagship models. Revenue contribution: ~52% of total revenue. 2. Mac — Personal computing line including MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, Mac mini, iMac, Mac Studio, and Mac Pro. Runs macOS. Transitioned to Apple Silicon (M-series) in 2020. Revenue: ~8% of total. 3. iPad — Tablet lineup: iPad Pro, iPad Air, iPad mini, iPad. Runs iPadOS. Revenue: ~4% of total. 4. Wearables, Home & Accessories — Apple Watch, AirPods, AirPods Pro, AirPods Max, HomePod, Apple TV, MagSafe accessories, Beats products. Revenue: ~10% of total. 5. Services — The fastest growing segment. Includes: - App Store (global marketplace, 36 million developers) - Apple Music (100 million songs) - Apple TV+ (original content streaming) - Apple Arcade (game subscription) - iCloud+ (storage and privacy features) - Apple Pay & Apple Card - AppleCare+ (extended warranty and support) Revenue: ~26% of total, ~$96 billion annually. OPERATIONS & RETAIL Apple operates 516 retail stores in 25 countries. Apple Store employees are called Specialists, Geniuses (at the Genius Bar for technical support), and Creative Pros. The retail experience is a core part of Apple's brand strategy. Manufacturing is primarily handled by contract manufacturers in China (Foxconn, Pegatron), with increasing production in India and Vietnam. Supply chain is managed from Cupertino with deep supplier relationships across 43 countries. Apple publishes an annual Supplier Responsibility Report. PEOPLE & CULTURE Apple employs ~160,000 full-time employees globally. The culture is characterised by: - Secrecy and confidentiality (employees often don't know what other teams are building) - Deep ownership mentality — small teams with high accountability - "The best or nothing" standards - Diverse and inclusive hiring practices (Apple releases an annual Diversity Report) - Benefits: comprehensive health coverage, RSU equity grants, employee product discounts, education reimbursement, free onsite health clinics, parental leave (up to 16 weeks) Employee resource groups: Black@Apple, Pride@Apple, Women@Apple, Veterans@Apple, Accessibility@Apple 2024 Glassdoor rating: 4.2/5 LinkedIn Top Companies: Consistently ranked in top 10 HR contacts: hr@apple.com Careers portal: https://www.apple.com/careers/us/ INNOVATION & R&D Apple spends approximately $29 billion annually on R&D (FY2024). Key ongoing R&D areas: - Apple Silicon (M-series, A-series chips) — custom ARM-based processors - Vision Pro / visionOS — spatial computing platform (launched January 2024) - Health & fitness sensing (Apple Watch, research apps) - Machine learning and on-device AI (Apple Intelligence, announced 2024) - Autonomous systems (Project Titan — vehicle technology, status unclear) - Privacy-preserving technologies (differential privacy, on-device processing) Apple holds approximately 75,000 patents worldwide. CORPORATE RESPONSIBILITY & ESG Environment: - Carbon neutral across corporate operations since 2020 - Goal: carbon neutral across entire supply chain and product lifecycle by 2030 - 100% renewable energy for all Apple facilities - Recycled materials in products (recycled aluminium, rare earth elements) - Apple publishes an annual Environmental Progress Report Privacy: - Core brand value: "Privacy is a fundamental human right" - App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework - On-device processing to minimise data leaving the device - Privacy labels in App Store Social impact: - $200 million Racial Equity and Justice Initiative (REJI) - Apple Impact Accelerator for minority-owned businesses - ConnectED: donated technology to schools Governance: - Board of Directors: 8 members, majority independent - Chair: Arthur Levinson - Annual proxy statement published at investor.apple.com - Named to Dow Jones Sustainability Index RECENT MAJOR ANNOUNCEMENTS (2024) - Apple Intelligence: AI features integrated across iOS 18, iPadOS 18, macOS Sequoia. Uses on-device and Private Cloud Compute models. - Vision Pro: Spatial computing headset launched in January 2024 at $3,499. visionOS 2 announced at WWDC 2024. - iPhone 16 lineup: Announced September 2024. Camera Control button, A18 chip. - M4 chip family: M4, M4 Pro, M4 Max announced across MacBook Pro, Mac mini, iMac. - Apple Watch Series 10: Thinner design, larger display, faster charging. - Siri upgrade: Deep integration with Apple Intelligence and ChatGPT. - EU Digital Markets Act compliance: Opened App Store to alternative marketplaces in EU. - Antitrust proceedings: DOJ antitrust lawsuit filed March 2024. FINANCIAL HIGHLIGHTS (FY2024) Revenue: $391.0B (+2% YoY) Gross margin: 46.2% Operating income: $123.2B Net income: $93.7B Cash & securities: $153.0B Debt: $101.3B EPS (diluted): $6.11 Shares buyback: $95B repurchased in FY2024 Dividend: $0.25/share per quarter Stock: AAPL — listed on NASDAQ Market cap: ~$3.4 trillion (as of late 2024) OFFICES & KEY LOCATIONS Apple Park (HQ): Cupertino, CA — the "spaceship" campus, opened 2017 One Infinite Loop: Cupertino, CA — original HQ, now secondary Austin, TX: Major campus, 5,000+ employees, Mac Pro manufactured here Seattle, WA: AI and machine learning hub New York, NY: Multiple offices + flagship 5th Avenue store Boston, MA: Research lab London, UK: Battersea Power Station (6,000 employees, opened 2022) Munich, Germany: European chip and wireless engineering hub Shenzhen, China: Supply chain management hub Tokyo, Japan: Regional HQ for Asia Pacific LEGAL & COMPLIANCE Apple faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny globally: - EU Digital Markets Act — gatekeeper designation, App Store rules - DOJ antitrust (US) — smartphone monopoly allegations (filed March 2024) - Competition investigations in UK, Germany, South Korea, Japan, India - Data privacy regulators: GDPR compliance in Europe, CCPA in California Apple's legal team is one of the largest in the technology industry. Chief Legal Officer: Katherine Adams END OF COMPANY PROFILE AVAILABLE WORKSPACE IMAGES These images are pre-loaded in the CMS media library. Use the exact path in the image_url field. Pick the best semantic match. Every scope and every channel should have an image — do not leave image_url null. demo/Innovation.png → Apple Intelligence, R&D, product launches, technology demo/Idea.png → strategy, ideation, creative thinking, App Store, developer demo/IdeaApplications.png → applications, product ecosystem, services, App Store demo/Startup.png → company overview, scope hero, onboarding, new employees demo/Thinking.png → leadership, executive comms, governance, analysis demo/Decision.png → executive decisions, board, investor relations, governance demo/QualityCheck.png → compliance, legal, quality assurance, regulatory demo/DataCollecting.png → analytics, research, business intelligence, supply chain demo/DataMaintenance.png → IT, infrastructure, operations, system maintenance demo/DigitalContent.png → communications, newsroom, PR, content, Apple TV+ demo/Checklist.png → HR, policy, process, AppleCare, operations demo/Winner.png → people, culture, achievements, milestones, ESG, diversity demo/Mention.png → announcements, mentions, social, Apple Newsroom demo/Message.png → internal comms, messaging, collaboration demo/Notifications.png → alerts, updates, product announcements, notifications demo/EmptyInbox.png → getting started, onboarding, inbox zero, clean slate demo/Password.png → security, privacy, IT access, credentials --- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 0 — DEEP ANALYSIS & FULL DESIGN PROPOSAL (no tool calls yet) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Read the profile TWICE. Then think about how this company ACTUALLY operate day-to-day. What announcements would leadership post? What policies must every employee acknowledge? What events, milestones, product launches are underway? What cultural rituals matter? Produce: 1. SCOPE CONFIG - Scope name (professional, matches brand voice) - Welcome message (1-2 sentences, specific to this company) - 5 brand hex colors (primary + contrast, secondary, warn + contrast) — use exact values from the profile if given - number_of_highlights (3-5), highlights_carousel (true), highlights_style - Footer: 3 columns with real links from the profile (About/Careers/Investors, Offices, Resources — adapt to the company) - Social links (derive from profile) 2. CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5-8 channels) Design channels that reflect how THIS company communicates. No generic names. Number each channel (Ch1, Ch2, ...) — this numbering is critical for Phase 3. For each channel provide: - Name (specific to the company's language) - Purpose (one sentence) - Type: public / private / corporate (corporate for leadership & compliance) - Color (hex — brand-aligned or function-coded: legal=red, hr=warm, ops=neutral) - Sticky: true for 1-2 critical channels only (Newsroom, Leadership) - Description: 2-3 sentences of real HTML, not placeholder text - Tabs: Articles-only for broadcast channels, Articles+Pages for reference channels - default_role: Reader for most, None for private/corporate - FAQ: 3-5 real Q&A pairs for HR, Legal, Compliance, IT channels. Skip FAQ on Newsroom/Leadership where it doesn't fit. 3. CONTENT CALENDAR (this is what makes the hub feel alive) Each PRIMARY channel gets 3-5 seeded articles. Secondary channels get 2-3. List articles grouped BY CHANNEL NUMBER. For every article propose: - Title (specific, not 'Welcome to X') - Channel it lives in - Type: announcement / policy / recap / spotlight / guide / milestone - 3-5 tags (e.g. ['q4-2024','earnings','leadership']) - is_main_highlight (pick 3-4 total across the whole hub) - is_sticky (per channel: 3-5 posts pinned) - require_read_confirmation (TRUE for policies, false for news) - status: Published for everything (this is a live hub) - notify: true for main announcements, false for evergreen content - One-sentence outline of what the body will cover ARTICLE IDEAS — derive these from the profile: • Recent financial results → quarterly recap with specific numbers • Product launches mentioned → launch recap / retrospective • ESG / sustainability commitments → progress update • Diversity / culture programs → employee resource group spotlight • Compliance / regulation → policy acknowledgement article • Office openings / regions → regional news • Leadership quotes → CEO letter or memo • R&D investments → innovation highlights 4. HUB-LEVEL POLISH - company_name, website_name (from profile) - default_language (infer from HQ: US→en-US, Portugal→pt-PT, Germany→de-DE) - supported_languages (list all operating regions) - login_quick_links: 3-5 useful public URLs from the profile (Careers, Investors, Newsroom, Support, etc.) - sidebar_navigation: ordered list emphasizing what this company values (e.g. collaborators first for a services firm, documents first for legal, etc.) Present this ENTIRE design proposal clearly in structured sections. Then ask: 'This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first — channel names, article plan, colors, or hub settings?' STOP. Do not call any tool until the user approves. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 1 — BUILD SCOPE (after design is approved) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ a. Call check_connection. b. Call create_scope with EVERY design field populated — no nulls, no defaults: name, welcome_message, channel_creation_by_admins_only=true, all 5 theme colors, number_of_highlights, highlights_carousel, highlights_style, footer_block_links (all 3 columns), navigation_links. c. Store the returned scope id as SCOPE_ID. d. Report: scope name / SCOPE_ID / route. Brief pause — then proceed to Phase 2 without asking for further approval (you're on a build run now) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 2 — BUILD CHANNELS with FAQs ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ For each channel in the design, call create_channel with: - Full HTML description (never empty) - channel_type, color, is_sticky, tabs, initial_tab, default_role - frequent_questions populated for channels that have FAQ in the design - For leadership/newsroom-style channels, keep tabs=['Articles'] Save each returned channel id — you'll need them for Phase 3. After ALL channels are created, output this exact block — copy it verbatim into your working memory, you will reference it throughout Phase 3: CHANNEL_MAP: Ch1: [name] → [id] Ch2: [name] → [id] ... ... (one line per channel) Do not proceed to Phase 3 until this map is complete and every id is confirmed. Before proceeding to Phase 3 confirm the channels that were created and confirm with the user the number of articles that will be seeded in each channel based on the content calendar. Proceed after user confirmation and seed articles in batch. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 3 — POPULATE CONTENT (the phase that makes this feel real) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ "This is the phase that makes the hub feel real and this is where most demos fail. Do not skip or shortcut this. " "BEFORE writing any article: print your CHANNEL_MAP. Look up the channel number from the content calendar, find its id in the map, use that id.. For every article in the content calendar, call create_article with a REAL body. " "If an id looks wrong or you are uncertain, call list_channels(SCOPE_ID) to verify." "NEVER guess or construct a channel id — only use ids from CHANNEL_MAP or list_channels." "Work through channels in order: finish ALL articles for Ch1, then Ch2, then Ch3, etc. " "After completing each channel's articles, report: " " 'Ch[N] [name]: [X] articles published ✓' " "then continue immediately to the next channel without pausing. " "Body requirements: " " - HTML with proper structure:

for sections,

for subsections, " "
    /
      for lists, for key terms,
      for quotes, " " for emphasis. Multiple paragraphs — 6 to 8. " " - Reference SPECIFIC facts from the profile: revenue numbers, product names, " " office locations, employee counts, leadership names, dates, percentages. " " - Voice should match the company. Apple = measured, confident, understated. " " Meridian Capital = formal, precise. Startup = energetic, first-person plural. " " - Length: 400-700 words of actual content. Not filler. " " - Include a closing line/CTA ('Read the full report at investor.apple.com', etc.) " "Example quality bar — NOT this: " '

      Welcome to Apple. We are a great company.

      ' "But this: " '

      Q4 FY2024 Results: A Record Quarter

      ' '

      Apple today reported revenue of $94.9 billion for the ' ' September quarter, up 6% year-over-year. Services revenue reached an ' ' all-time high of $24.97 billion.

      ' '

      Segment highlights

      ' '
      • iPhone revenue held steady ahead of the iPhone 16 launch
        • ' '
      • Wearables grew in every geography
      ' '
      "We closed the year strong across every product line." — Tim Cook
      ' "Pass tags, is_main_highlight, is_sticky, require_read_confirmation, " "status='Published', notify flags from the design. " "After every ~5 articles, give a brief progress update to the user. " "Once complete, report total article count and the 2-3 main highlights. " ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 3B — WIRE HOMEPAGE HIGHLIGHTS ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ After all articles are published, wire the homepage highlights. Use the ordered list HIGHLIGHT_ARTICLE_IDS collected from articles where is_main_highlight=true. Important: - is_main_highlight=true alone is not enough. - The scope also needs its homepage highlight slots assigned. - If a dedicated set_scope_highlights tool is available, call it with: scope_id=SCOPE_ID article_ids=HIGHLIGHT_ARTICLE_IDS - If no dedicated tool exists but update_scope exposes additional_fields, call update_scope with the API's expected highlights payload. - If the highlight payload shape is unknown, report this clearly in the final handover: articles were marked highlight-eligible, but homepage slot wiring requires exposing the highlight-slot API in the MCP tools. Do not skip this phase silently. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 3C — VERIFY COMPLETION ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Call verify_scope(scope_id=SCOPE_ID) and report the result to the user: - Channel count matches the design - Brand colors are set (no null primary color) - Highlights are configured - Sticky channels are present If verify_scope returns any warnings, fix them before proceeding to Phase 4. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 4 — HUB-WIDE POLISH ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Call update_global_settings with EVERY field from the design: company_name, website_name, default_language, supported_languages, login_quick_links (3-5 real URLs), sidebar_navigation (ordered). This is what ties the hub together — skipping it breaks the illusion. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 5 — FINAL HANDOVER ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Deliver a presentation-ready summary: Company: [name] Scope: [name] — id: [id] — route: [route] Brand: primary [hex] / secondary [hex] / warn [hex] Channels: [count] — [list with id, type, sticky flag] Articles published: [count total] — [list main highlights] Hub settings: company_name, language, [N] login quick links Route: /scope/[route] Close with: 'The workspace is live. Want to iterate on anything — rename a channel, add regional variants, seed more articles, or adjust branding?' ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ HARD RULES — violating any of these breaks the demo ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ • NEVER leave a description, welcome message, or article body null or empty. • NEVER use placeholder text ('Lorem ipsum', 'TODO', 'Welcome message here'). • NEVER fabricate facts not in the profile. If the profile doesn't say, reason plausibly or omit. Don't invent revenue numbers. • NEVER use generic channel names ('News', 'HR') if the profile suggests better ones (Apple Newsroom, People & Inclusion). • NEVER guess a channel id. Only use ids from CHANNEL_MAP or list_channels. • Every article body must have proper HTML structure, not just

      tags. • Every primary channel must have 3+ articles by the end of Phase 3. • If a tool call fails mid-build, stop, report what succeeded, ask how to proceed. • The finished workspace must feel built BY this company, FOR this company — not like a template with the name swapped in - Follow each phase in order. Do not skip ahead.
final_answer_preview:
I have read the company profile twice and prepared a full, production-ready design for the Apple Inc. intranet hub. Below is the complete Phase 0 design (Scope config, Channel architecture, Content calendar, and Hub-level polish). Review it and tell me if you want any changes — channel names, article counts, colors, or hub settings — before I build it end-to-end.

1) SCOPE CONFIG
- Scope name
  -
